title: Winthrop man charged with Social Security fraud, concealment
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The man allegedly concealed a person’s death from the government so he could fraudulently receive their retirement benefits.
By Darin Zullo April 12, 2026 | 11:00 AM 1 minute to read A Winthrop man was charged Friday for allegedly committing Social Security fraud for over four years, according to prosecutors.
Kenneth Riel, 51, was indicted on one count of receiving stolen government money or property and one count of Social Security fraud-concealment, the U.

title: Former VA employee charged with fraudulently obtaining COVID-19 benefits
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The Brockton man allegedly applied for loans using fraudulent tax documents and making false claims about his revenues.

By Darin Zullo April 12, 2026 | 10:02 AM 1 minute to read A former Veterans Affairs employee was charged in federal court Thursday for allegedly fraudulently obtaining COVID-19 benefits, prosecutors said.
Jesus Abreu, 38, of Brockton, was indicted on three counts of wire fraud, the U.

title: Chelsea man sentenced for stealing mail, fraudulently depositing checks
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The man was one of nine members of the “Mission Hill Gang” involved in the scheme and charged in 2024, prosecutors said.

By Darin Zullo April 12, 2026 | 9:22 AM 1 minute to read A Chelsea man was sentenced in federal court Friday for his role in a scheme to fraudulently deposit checks he stole from the mail, according to prosecutors.
Josman Romero-Delgado, 24, pleaded guilty in November 2025 to charges of conspiracy to commit bank fraud, bank fraud, and conspiracy to steal and possess stolen mail, the U.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Colman Domingo hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
